name: From Granada via Vilobí del Penedés – circular route around Villafranca del Penedés
name_original: Desde la Granada por Vilobí del Penedés – ruta circular en los alrededores de Villafranca del Penedés
region_intro: Hiking around La Granada offers routes through the Penedès wine region, characterized by extensive vineyards and gentle rolling hills. The area features a mix of agricultural landscapes and historical points of interest, such as the Castle of La Granada. Trails often follow quiet country paths, providing accessible options for various skill levels. Best hiking trails around La Granada The most popular hiking route is Castle of La Granada loop from Alt Penedès, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 19 minutes to complete, passing the historic Castle of La Granada. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cal Ferret Pine Park loop from Santa Fe del Penedès, an easy 4.6 miles (7.5 km) path exploring the Cal Ferret Pine Park. Local hikers also love the View of the Vineyard loop from les Cabanyes, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through vineyard landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Hiking around La Granada is defined by vineyard paths, gentle hills, and historical sites. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in La Granada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1800 hikers have used komoot to explore La Granada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around La Granada?
A: There are over 240 hiking routes available around La Granada, offering a diverse range of experiences through the Penedès wine region. These include options for various skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in La Granada?
A: Yes, La Granada offers nearly 100 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31569728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Granada loop from Alt Penedès&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.1 miles (5.0 km) and takes about 1 hour 19 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around La Granada?
A: The terrain around La Granada is characterized by the Penedès wine region, featuring extensive vineyards and gentle rolling hills. Trails often follow quiet country paths through agricultural landscapes, with historical points of interest like the Castle of La Granada along the way.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in La Granada?
A: Many of the trails around La Granada are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17513951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cal Ferret Pine Park loop from Santa Fe del Penedès&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.6 miles (7.5 km) circular path exploring a local pine park.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails around La Granada?
A: Beyond the vineyards, you can discover various points of interest. Highlights include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3922795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pla dels Albats Necropolis and Chapel Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, and scenic viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravinya La Bardera&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3312743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gran Pèlag of Vilobí del Penedès&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: While many routes are easy to moderate, there are also more challenging options. For a moderate hike, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14897857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crossing of the Rovira stream – El Pla del Penedès loop from Santa Fe del Penedès&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 7.1 miles (11.4 km) and involves some elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Granada?
A: The Penedès region, where La Granada is located, generally offers pleasant hiking conditions in spring and autumn when temperatures are mild. Summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter is usually mild but can be damp.

Q: Are the hiking trails around La Granada suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails around La Granada are suitable for families, especially the easier, flatter routes that wind through vineyards and quiet country paths. These provide a gentle introduction to hiking for younger adventurers. Look for routes described as 'easy' for the best family experience.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Granada?
A: The hiking routes in La Granada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the beautiful vineyard landscapes, the gentle hills, and the historical sites encountered along the paths.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer particularly good views of the vineyards?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its vineyards. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6933328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Vineyard loop from les Cabanyes&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail specifically designed to showcase the stunning vineyard landscapes.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in La Granada?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. In general, smaller towns and villages in the Penedès region often have public parking areas. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot, as users often provide tips on parking locations.

Q: Can I find any hidden gems or less-trafficked routes?
A: While popular routes are well-loved, the extensive network of over 240 trails means there are many quieter paths to explore. Look for routes that venture slightly off the main villages, often leading through less-frequented agricultural areas or smaller pine groves, offering a more secluded experience.
tips: The landscape of the Penedès plain is characterized by its undulating relief, with gentle slopes only interrupted by the passage of numerous streams, rivers and torrents. The cultivation of the vineyard is omnipresent, and gives it character, along with its peculiar distribution of small inhabited nuclei, isolated farmhouses and small towns. Numerous local and regional roads cross it, making it a perfect setting for cycling tourism.

L'Avellà is a small inhabited nucleus, belonging to the municipality of Font-rubí. It is an obligatory step on the ascent to Alto de Font-rubí, one of the classic cycling ascents of l'Alt Penedès. From Guardiola de Font-rubí, a double row of banana trees welcomes us to this picturesque corner of the Penedès, surrounded by vineyards and country houses.

Among these constructions, the Villa Josefa Formosa stands out, a building with a square floor plan and covered with an Arab tile pavilion built in 1902. It is currently renovated and works as a wine tasting room for the Heretat Mont-Rubí winery, which is based in the same locality.

Another singular house is Can Gol, a farmhouse built at the beginning of the 20th century that stands out for its noucentista style, especially for the use in all the openings of its façade of blue tile frames, alternating designs of white and blue squares and a border of blue rhombuses. . The frontal figure of Sant Jordi and the dragon stands out, also made from a tile mosaic.

This building with a square floor plan and a pavilion covered with Arabic tiles was built in 1902. It is currently renovated and functions as a wine tasting room for the Heretat Mont-Rubí winery, which is based in the same town.

This particular farmhouse was built at the beginning of the 20th century and stands out for its Noucentista style, especially for the use of blue tile frames in all the openings of its façade, alternating designs of white and blue squares and a border of blue rhombuses. The frontal figure of Sant Jordi and the dragon stands out, also made from a mosaic of tiles.

This mountain pass can be crowned by three slopes: from La Llacuna it is 4.1 kilometers long and has an average gradient of 4.8%, from Sant Martí Sarroca, 14.1 kilometers and 3.6%, and from the diversion of the Pontons road, 6.1 kilometers and 5.6%. All the slopes are very calm, with very little traffic. The slope from the Pontons turnoff has the road surface in the worst condition, since it is a paved road while the other two are well paved roads.

Church in the municipality of Torrelles de Foix, with Baroque architecture.

Find a public fountain where you can fill up water.

This section of paved neighborhood road allows you to avoid the busiest section of road (BP-2121) that crosses the town of Sant Martí Sarroca and offers a sample of the typical Penedès landscape: vineyards and small farmhouses in a quiet environment that is perfect for practicing of cycling tourism.
This church began to be built at the beginning of the 20th century, with a style that imitated the Romanesque.
